chapter_title.en: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them

text: 700 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «مَنْ كَنَسَ اَلْمَسْجِدَ يَوْمَ اَلْخَمِيسِ وَ لَيْلَةَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ فَأَخْرَجَ مِنْهُ مِنَ اَلتُّرَابِ مَا يُذَرُّ فِي اَلْعَيْنِ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى لَهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.700 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Whoever sweeps the mosque on Thursday and the night of Jumu’ah (Thursday night) and removes from it even the dust that could irritate the eye, Allah (swt), the Exalted, will forgive them."
